title: For Loops
description: Repeat actions a known number of times.

slides.title: The Need to Repeat 🔁
slides.content:
Computers are fantastic at doing repetitive tasks instantly.

If you need to print 'Hello' 5 times, you use a Loop instead of writing `console.log` 5 times.

slides.title: The For Loop
slides.content: A `for` loop has 3 parts: Initialization (`let i = 0`), Condition (`i < 5`), and Incrementing (`i++`).

quizData.question: What does `i++` do in the loop?
quizData.explanation: `i++` is a shortcut for `i = i + 1`, successfully moving the loop forward each time.

slides.title: Breaking the Loop 🔨
slides.content:
You can force a loop to completely stop early using the `break` keyword. 

This immediately terminates the entire loop.

slides.title: Skipping an Iteration 🦘
slides.content: You can skip the current loop cycle and move to the next one using the `continue` keyword.
